steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes show two key facts: syllable rate (SR) varied widely across the 17 languages, but information rate (IR) was roughly the same (~39 bits/sec) for all of them. The correct choice must capture this contrast between varying SR and steady IR.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Though some of the languages differed in number of syllables spoken per second, all 17 conveyed information at roughly the same rate.: Captures the core contrast from the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — The 2021 study determined the information rate (IR) of 17 languages in bits of information conveyed per second.: Ignores the finding about information rate, which is the more surprising result. ✗
  • Choice C — Vietnamese had the sixteenth fastest syllable rate, lower than that of Spanish, which had the second fastest; however, Spanish had the lower information rate of the two.: Contradicts the notes, which say IR was roughly constant. ✗
  • Choice D — Researchers found that information was conveyed more quickly in Spanish, at 7.7 syllables per second, than in Vietnamese, at 5.3 syllables per second.: Introduces information beyond what the notes contain. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
